headlineStat: UK financial services firms received 1.85m complaints in 2025 H1.
supportingDetail: This was a 3.6% increase from 2024 H2. Since 2021 H1, complaints have stayed relatively constant between 1.7m and 2.0m.
sourceNote: UK financial services complaints volume context.
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: market/problem scale
Design Partner Pack: why complaints are a strong wedge
Sample Pilot Report: context for complaints pilot
caution: Do not claim Corentis reduces complaint volume. Frame this as evidence that complaints handling is a large operational problem.

headlineStat: Total redress in FCA complaints data was £283m in 2025 H1.
supportingDetail: This was a 20.0% increase on the 2024 H2 figure of £236m.
sourceNote: UK financial services complaints redress context.
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: commercial cost of poor outcomes
Assurance & Governance Summary: why evidence and controls matter
caution: Do not claim Corentis reduces redress by a specific percentage.

headlineStat: The Financial Ombudsman Service received 305,726 new complaints in 2024/25.
supportingDetail: This was the highest level for six years and 54% higher than 2023/24.
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: escalation pressure
Design Partner Pack: why a controlled complaints workflow is commercially relevant
caution: Do not imply Corentis replaces firms’ complaint handling obligations or ombudsman processes.

headlineStat: 26.4m UK adults had characteristics of vulnerability in May 2024.
supportingDetail: This represented 49% of all UK adults.
sourceName: Financial Lives 2024: Key findings from the FCA’s Financial Lives May 2024 survey
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: why vulnerable-customer handling matters
Design Partner Pack: why the first pilot should include vulnerable-customer signals
Control Matrix Example: vulnerable disclosure control row
Sample Pilot Report: sample scenario context
caution: Do not imply all vulnerable customers are complainants. Use this as evidence of the scale of the regulated duty-of-care context.

headlineStat: 7.3m UK adults were heavily burdened by domestic bills and/or credit commitments in May 2024.
supportingDetail: 4.5m adults were in financial difficulty because they had missed domestic bills and/or credit commitments in three or more of the previous six months.
sourceName: Financial Lives 2024: Key findings from the FCA’s Financial Lives May 2024 survey
sourceNote: UK financial difficulty and hardship context.
suggestedUse: Sample Pilot Report: hardship scenario
Control Matrix Example: financial difficulty / escalation row
Assurance & Governance Summary: why human review matters in sensitive cases
caution: Use in the context of hardship/vulnerability detection, not as a general market-size claim.

headlineStat: In around 1 in 5 recent contact cases, consumers found it difficult or were not able to get through to someone at their financial services provider.
sourceName: Financial Lives 2024: Key findings from the FCA’s Financial Lives May 2024 survey
sourceNote: UK financial services contact difficulty context.
suggestedUse: Design Partner Pack: customer experience problem
Assurance & Governance Summary: support and accessibility context
Investor Overview: operational pressure
caution: Do not claim Corentis is a contact-centre routing product. Frame this as part of the broader service-pressure environment.

headlineStat: Only 39% of adults had confidence in the UK financial services industry.
supportingDetail: 36% thought most financial firms are honest and transparent in the way they treat them.
sourceName: Financial Lives 2024: Key findings from the FCA’s Financial Lives May 2024 survey
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: trust gap
Why Corentis page/resource introduction if needed
Design Partner Pack: why explainability and evidence matter
caution: Do not claim Corentis directly increases consumer trust. Say evidence-led workflows can help firms demonstrate more controlled handling.

headlineStat: Firms must give consumers information they need, at the right time, presented in a way they can understand.
supportingDetail: FCA vulnerable-customer good practice also says firms should ensure communications are likely to be understood by the intended customers and consider the needs of consumers in vulnerable circumstances.
sourceName: Delivering good outcomes for customers in vulnerable circumstances
sourceNote: FCA vulnerable-customer and communications good-practice context.
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: regulatory expectation
Control Matrix Example: communication control rows
Sample Pilot Report: control objectives
caution: Do not present this as legal advice. Include the standard disclaimer.

headlineStat: 88% of respondents in McKinsey’s 2025 global survey reported regular AI use in at least one business function.
supportingDetail: Only approximately one-third reported that their companies had begun scaling AI programmes.
sourceName: The State of AI: Global Survey 2025
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: market timing
Runtime Checkpoint Explainer: why runtime control is becoming urgent
caution: This is global cross-industry survey data, not UK financial-services-only data.

headlineStat: 23% of respondents said their organisations are scaling an agentic AI system somewhere in the enterprise.
supportingDetail: A further 39% said they had begun experimenting with AI agents.
sourceName: The State of AI: Global Survey 2025
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: why Corentis is timed around AI agents
Runtime Checkpoint Explainer: why a checkpoint-before-action layer matters
caution: Use as evidence of agentic AI momentum, not proof of Corentis demand.

headlineStat: 51% of respondents from organisations using AI said their organisations had seen at least one negative consequence.
supportingDetail: Nearly one-third of all respondents reported consequences stemming from AI inaccuracy.
sourceName: The State of AI: Global Survey 2025
sourceNote: Global AI risk and consequence context.
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: need for validation, review and controls
Investor Overview: risk side of AI adoption
caution: Do not say Corentis prevents all negative consequences. Say it is designed to add checkpoints, review and evidence around sensitive workflow actions.

headlineStat: 63% of breached organisations lacked AI governance policies to manage AI or prevent shadow AI.
supportingDetail: IBM also reported that 97% of organisations with an AI-related security incident lacked proper AI access controls.
sourceName: Cost of a Data Breach Report 2025
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: governance gap
Runtime Checkpoint Explainer: why oversight must be operational
Investor Overview: AI control layer opportunity
caution: This is security/breach research. Use it to support the broader governance-gap argument, not to claim Corentis is a cybersecurity breach-prevention product.

supportingDetail: The FCA also secured an estimated £442.3m for consumers and investors through redress schemes or settlements agreed as part of enforcement outcomes or civil proceedings.
sourceNote: General regulated-sector enforcement and redress context.
suggestedUse: Investor Overview: commercial/regulatory cost context
Assurance & Governance Summary: why evidence and control matter
caution: Do not imply all penalties relate to AI or complaints. Use as general regulated-sector enforcement context.

headlineStat: FCA fined TSB £10.9m for failing to ensure customers in arrears were treated fairly.
supportingDetail: TSB paid £99.9m in redress to 232,849 affected customers.
sourceName: FCA fines TSB £10.9m over treatment of customers in financial difficulty
sourceNote: Financial difficulty treatment and control-failure example.
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: real-world systems/control failure example
Control Matrix Example: why fair-treatment checkpoints matter
caution: Do not imply TSB used AI or that Corentis would have prevented this. Use as evidence that poor treatment, weak systems and poor controls can have major financial consequences.

headlineStat: FCA fined Volkswagen Finance £5.4m for failing to treat customers in financial difficulty fairly.
supportingDetail: Volkswagen Finance agreed to pay over £21.5m in redress to around 110,000 customers.
sourceName: FCA fines Volkswagen Finance £5.4m over treatment of customers in financial difficulty
sourceNote: Financial difficulty treatment and control-failure example.
suggestedUse: Assurance & Governance Summary: systems/control failure example
Sample Pilot Report: hardship-control context
Control Matrix Example: financial difficulty treatment row
caution: Do not imply Volkswagen Finance used AI or that Corentis would have prevented this. Use as evidence of the commercial consequences of poor customer-treatment controls.

headlineStat: 53% of bad experiences result in customers cutting spending.
supportingDetail: Qualtrics also reports that first-call resolution makes customers 2.1x more likely to recommend.
suggestedUse: Design Partner Pack: customer-experience commercial impact
Investor Overview: CX-to-commercial-impact bridge
caution: Vendor research. Use lightly and clearly as customer-experience context, not primary regulatory evidence.

headlineStat: 72% of customers say it is important to know if they are communicating with an AI agent.
supportingDetail: Only 17% are comfortable with an AI agent making financial decisions for them.
sourceName: State of the AI Connected Customer
sourceDate: 2026 page accessed / report current at access
sourceNote: Vendor AI trust and customer expectations context.
suggestedUse: Runtime Checkpoint Explainer: why AI transparency and action boundaries matter
Design Partner Pack: why sensitive financial workflows need human review
Investor Overview: AI trust gap
caution: Vendor research and global consumer/business-buyer sample. Use as trust context, not UK-regulatory evidence.
